agenda_high: WHISPERS: {advisor} seen dining with people who don't like you. Twice.
SOURCES: {advisor}'s calendar has gaps even their assistant can't explain.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} described the administration as 'temporary'. Make of that what you will.
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} has been unusually well-dressed for someone with no public events.
WHISPERS: {advisor} is drafting memos that read suspiciously like a ransom note.
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} was seen measuring the curtains in the governor's office.
SOURCES: {advisor} keeps referring to themselves in the third person during closed-door meetings.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} telling a potted plant that the administration is a sinking ship.
WHISPERS: {advisor} has a private line installed that bypasses the switchboard. Who is calling?
SOURCES: {advisor} recently purchased a book titled 'How to Survive a Coup'.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} laughing hysterically while shredding documents at 3 AM.
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} is taking meetings with people who wear expensive suits and cheap cologne.
WHISPERS: {advisor} refused to drink the coffee provided at the last cabinet meeting.
SOURCES: {advisor} is practicing their 'shocked and appalled' face in the mirror.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} asking hypothetically about the extradition treaties of {city.name}.
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} has suddenly become very interested in off-shore banking regulations.
WHISPERS: {advisor} was spotted feeding pigeons and muttering about treason.
SOURCES: {advisor} is keeping a diary. The governor should probably be worried.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} suggesting that democracy is just a 'phase'.
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} has stopped blinking during staff briefings.
WHISPERS: {advisor} ordered new stationary with a suspiciously regal crest.
SOURCES: {advisor} is the only one in the building who knows where the fire exits actually lead.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} humming a funeral dirge outside the governor's door.
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} has been spending an awful lot of time at the embassy.
WHISPERS: {advisor} keeps mentioning a 'contingency plan' that involves a helicopter.
SOURCES: {advisor} was seen shaking hands with the opposition leader in a dark alley.
OVERHEARD: {advisor} saying 'I give it a week, tops.'
RUMOR MILL: {advisor} is hoarding office supplies as if preparing for a siege.
WHISPERS: {advisor} smiled today. It terrified everyone.
SOURCES: {advisor} has a resignation letter framed on their desk, just waiting for a date.
romance: SEEN: a certain cabinet member leaving city hall well after midnight. Working late, surely.
BLIND ITEM: which senior official's car was parked where it shouldn't be? Our lips are sealed. Loosely.
SOCIETY PAGE: two familiar faces, one restaurant booth, zero official business.
BLIND ITEM: which two bitter political rivals were sharing a single dessert at an overpriced bistro?
SEEN: a discarded tie on the floor of the archive room. Someone was doing heavy research.
SOCIETY PAGE: the tension between those two deputies isn't strictly professional.
BLIND ITEM: a bouquet of roses was delivered to an office known only for budgetary cuts.
SEEN: two hands lingering a bit too long over a shared zoning permit.
SOCIETY PAGE: which press secretary is aggressively screening calls from a certain wealthy developer?
BLIND ITEM: lip gloss found on a microphone in the briefing room. We have our suspects.
SEEN: a mysterious individual sneaking out of the back exit of city hall, adjusting their collar.
SOCIETY PAGE: love is in the air, or maybe it's just the smell of municipal corruption and cheap perfume.
BLIND ITEM: an expense report claimed a romantic dinner for two was a 'strategic alignment meeting'.
SEEN: a crumpled love note left in the copy machine, written on official letterhead.
SOCIETY PAGE: which high-ranking official was buying jewelry entirely unrelated to an anniversary?
BLIND ITEM: heavy breathing heard on an unmuted conference call. The committee adjourned early.
SEEN: two figures locked in a passionate embrace behind the recycling bins.
SOCIETY PAGE: a sudden reshuffle of seating arrangements at the gala tells a very sordid story.
BLIND ITEM: someone left a very personal item in the executive private washroom.
SEEN: long glances exchanged across the aisle during the budget debate.
SOCIETY PAGE: the real reason a certain ordinance passed? A private agreement made over cocktails.
BLIND ITEM: which department head is suddenly wearing far too much cologne?
SEEN: a romantic walk along the riverfront, flanked by two nervous security details.
SOCIETY PAGE: the most successful negotiation this week happened in a hotel lobby, off the clock.
BLIND ITEM: a suspiciously matching pair of hickeys in the legal department.
SEEN: passing a very un-bureaucratic note during a tedious powerpoint presentation.
SOCIETY PAGE: a quiet resignation follows a very loud argument in a downtown restaurant.
BLIND ITEM: which two officials take their breaks at the exact same time, in opposite directions?
SEEN: a very affectionate goodbye in the parking garage, captured entirely by security cameras.
SOCIETY PAGE: romance blooms in the most unlikely of municipal departments.
pact: FOLLOW THE MONEY: a department's numbers are too clean. Auditors love clean numbers. They frame them.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why does one office keep declining routine reviews?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a certain contractor always bids exactly one dollar below the competition. Magic.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: where do the funds for the phantom road repair project actually go?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: an offshore account was just linked to a prominent local charity. How generous.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why did the ethics committee quietly shred their own meeting minutes?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a developer bought swampland and suddenly got it zoned for high-rises. A miracle.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: who authorized the purchase of thirty luxury sedans for the sanitation department?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a shell company with no employees just won the city's largest IT contract.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why is the mayor's nephew suddenly a highly paid consultant on bridge integrity?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: cash missing from the pension fund was written off as an 'accounting quirk'.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: what exactly is stored in the warehouse the city rents for ten grand a month?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: the cost of paperclips at city hall has skyrocketed by four hundred percent.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why are all the lucrative contracts awarded at 4:59 PM on Fridays?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a prominent official paid off their mortgage the day after a major zoning vote.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: who keeps silencing the whistleblowers in the health department?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a newly paved road leads directly to a private estate and nowhere else.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why did the city purchase a snowplow in the middle of a drought?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: an anonymous donation just covered the legal fees of a very guilty-looking deputy.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: where are the missing hard drives from the planning commission?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: the budget for 'miscellaneous expenses' is now larger than the education budget.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why does the chief auditor drive a car they clearly cannot afford?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a local union leader suddenly owns a yacht. It is named 'Collective Bargaining'.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: who approved the demolition of a historic building at 2 AM?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: the city's investment portfolio consists entirely of speculative crypto assets.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why are the surveillance tapes from election night completely blank?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: a highly paid expert testified for exactly three seconds to approve a billion-dollar deal.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: what happened to the emergency relief funds from last year's minor flood?
FOLLOW THE MONEY: the catering bill for the austerity commission is ironically astronomical.
QUESTION OF THE WEEK: why is the tax assessor so heavily invested in local real estate?
low_trust: MORALE WATCH: cabinet meetings reportedly 'brisk'. That's the polite word.

Insiders say {governor_title} spent the morning rereading their own approval numbers.

murmurs: EDITOR'S NOTE: we're hearing things. Nothing printable. Yet.
EDITOR'S NOTE: an interesting tip came across our desk. We are verifying.
EDITOR'S NOTE: people are talking. We are listening.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a curious discrepancy in the public record has caught our eye.
EDITOR'S NOTE: rumors are just rumors, until they aren't.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we've received an anonymous letter. It is compelling.
EDITOR'S NOTE: someone is nervous at city hall. We wonder why.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a quiet conversation in a dark corner was overheard.
EDITOR'S NOTE: there is a draft under the door of the mayor's office.
EDITOR'S NOTE: smoke usually means fire, but sometimes it just means a cover-up.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are pulling a few threads. Let's see what unravels.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a certain official's schedule is suddenly very vague.
EDITOR'S NOTE: our reporters are following a trail of breadcrumbs.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a small inconsistency in the budget is raising questions.
EDITOR'S NOTE: someone forgot to lock their filing cabinet.
EDITOR'S NOTE: the whisper network is buzzing with a familiar name.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a source tells us to look closer at yesterday's announcement.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are checking the math on the latest proposal. It doesn't add up.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a long-forgotten document has resurfaced.
EDITOR'S NOTE: someone is trying very hard to appear innocent.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are connecting a few dots. A picture is emerging.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a polite inquiry from our staff was met with uncharacteristic hostility.
EDITOR'S NOTE: the silence from certain quarters is deafening.
EDITOR'S NOTE: an interesting coincidence happened this week. Or was it?
EDITOR'S NOTE: a little bird told us a very big secret.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are doing some light digging in the archives.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a casual remark by a deputy revealed more than intended.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we note a sudden change in policy with great interest.
EDITOR'S NOTE: the official story has a few minor plot holes.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a shadow falls across the {city.government_body}.
watched: EDITOR'S NOTE: our newsroom has opened a file with your name on it, Governor. It's thin. We're patient.
EDITOR'S NOTE: our reporters are stationed outside your office. We have coffee. We can wait.
EDITOR'S NOTE: the file on your administration is growing, page by damning page.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we see you, Governor. Every misstep is being logged.
EDITOR'S NOTE: our eyes are fixed firmly on the {city.government_body}.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are compiling a list of broken promises. It is long.
EDITOR'S NOTE: the surveillance is not literal, but it is thorough.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are taking notes. Your legacy is being drafted in our newsroom.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a dedicated team is now assigned solely to your administration's blunders.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are watching the money, the meetings, and the mistakes.
EDITOR'S NOTE: our patience is vast, but our scrutiny is microscopic.
EDITOR'S NOTE: every word you say is being cross-referenced with reality.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we have a bulletin board covered in red string, and your picture is in the middle.
EDITOR'S NOTE: the spotlight is on you, and it is unflattering.
EDITOR'S NOTE: we are recording history as you ruin it.
EDITOR'S NOTE: a close watch is being kept on your closest allies.

crisis_window: WEATHER: storm system approaching {city.name}. The political kind. Expected within a day.
WEATHER: a massive front of incompetence is stalling over the city. Expect fallout by tomorrow.
WEATHER: hurricane-force scandals expected to make landfall in exactly 24 hours.
WEATHER: the pressure is dropping. A political explosion is imminent within the next turn.
WEATHER: heavy downpours of public outrage predicted for tomorrow morning.
WEATHER: a severe credibility drought will reach critical levels by daybreak.
WEATHER: dark clouds are gathering over {city.landmark}. Lightning strikes expected soon.
WEATHER: a cold front of reality is about to hit city hall. Dress warmly.

WEATHER: a tsunami of bad consequences is approaching the shore.
WEATHER: seismic activity detected in the polling numbers. Eruption expected shortly.

WEATHER: the climate in city hall has shifted from toxic to lethal.
WEATHER: a perfect storm of mismanagement and bad luck is converging right now.
WEATHER: gale-force winds of change threaten to topple the administration today.
WEATHER: an eclipse of the governor's sanity will be total by tomorrow.

WEATHER: a deep freeze is coming to the city's credit rating.
WEATHER: the atmospheric pressure in the cabinet room is nearing the bursting point.

WEATHER: a tidal wave of litigation is visible on the horizon.
WEATHER: expect a shower of resignations by the end of the turn.
WEATHER: the barometer of public trust has shattered completely.
WEATHER: severe thunderstorms of accountability are rolling in.
WEATHER: a meteor of truth is hurtling towards the governor's desk.
WEATHER: the political climate is changing, and you are going extinct.
deficit: MARKETS: city credit outlook 'comedic', say analysts. Payroll watch continues.
MARKETS: city bonds downgraded to 'junk', then downgraded again to 'radioactive waste'.
MARKETS: the municipal budget is currently held together by thoughts and prayers.
MARKETS: accountants weep openly in the streets as the deficit figures are released.
MARKETS: city hall proposes paying contractors in exposure and IOUs.
MARKETS: the treasury is empty. The mice have starved.
MARKETS: local currency is now worth less than the paper it's printed on.
MARKETS: the city's fiscal policy described by experts as 'a suicide pact'.
MARKETS: massive hole found in budget. Mayor attempts to fill it with rhetoric.
MARKETS: investors flee {city.name} faster than rats from a sinking ship.
MARKETS: the deficit has achieved sentience and is demanding tribute.
MARKETS: city planners suggest selling {city.landmark} to cover this month's payroll.
MARKETS: an audit reveals the city's emergency fund is just an empty jar of peanut butter.
MARKETS: financial ruin is no longer a risk, but a daily reality.

MARKETS: the word 'bankrupt' is being thrown around with terrifying frequency.
MARKETS: tax hikes imminent to cover the cost of the governor's mistakes.

MARKETS: the treasury's echo is getting louder every day.
MARKETS: default is no longer a dirty word; it's the only plan.
MARKETS: massive debt discovered. Governor blames previous administration, math.
MARKETS: the financial cliff was not a metaphor. We have driven off it.

MARKETS: the budget deficit is now visible from space.
MARKETS: rating agencies have run out of letters to downgrade us to.
MARKETS: the city's finances resemble a Ponzi scheme run by toddlers.
